name: Neon (Serverless Postgres)
pricing_notes: Free tier: 0.5GB storage, 1 project; Launch $19/mo; Scale $69/mo

auth.notes: Standard Postgres connection strings; API key for management API

performance.notes: Cold start on scale-to-zero ~1s; warm instances fast; HTTP API for serverless

agent_readiness.notes: Serverless Postgres with autoscaling and branching. Standard psycopg2/asyncpg connection (just use standard Postgres connection string). Neon REST API for management: create branches, databases. Database branching: instant copy-on-write branches. Scale to zero when idle. Autoscaling compute. Good for: dev/test databases, ephemeral environments, CI/CD test branches. HTTP API for serverless edge functions. Compatible with all Postgres clients (SQLAlchemy, Prisma, etc.).

gotchas: Scale-to-zero causes ~1s cold start on first query after idle period
Branching feature limited to branch per project (quota)
No persistent connections during scale-to-zero idle
Connection pooling via PgBouncer required for many short connections
best_when: Development and test databases with instant branching
Serverless/edge apps needing Postgres without managing instances
CI/CD pipelines needing fresh database branches per PR
Cost-optimized Postgres with autoscaling (pay for compute used)
avoid_when: Latency-sensitive apps sensitive to cold starts
Very large databases (Supabase/RDS more mature for >100GB)
summary: Neon is serverless Postgres with autoscaling, scale-to-zero, and instant database branching. Use standard psycopg2/asyncpg connection strings — fully Postgres-compatible. Branching enables per-PR test databases. HTTP API for management (create branches, databases). Autoscales to zero when idle (~1s cold start). Great for dev/test environments and serverless apps. Free tier available.
